Stucco Repair in Burnet County, TX

Burnet County's Hill Country landscape is beautiful, but the limestone terrain, intense UV exposure, and seasonal downpours create real challenges for stucco exteriors. Star Stucco of Austin repairs cracked, water-damaged, and deteriorating stucco on homes and businesses across Marble Falls, Burnet, Bertram, and the lakeside communities.

Get a Free EstimateCall (512) 706-9699

Hill Country Conditions and Your Stucco

Burnet County sits in the Texas Hill Country where the terrain shifts from rolling limestone hills to the shores of Lake Buchanan and Inks Lake. Homes built on limestone outcroppings experience minimal foundation settlement compared to clay-heavy areas to the east, but the rock itself presents challenges — shallow soils, difficult drainage, and the tendency for water to pool against walls during heavy rain.

The elevation and open terrain in Burnet County mean stucco surfaces take a relentless beating from UV radiation. Over time, this sun exposure breaks down the binders in the stucco, causing the surface to become chalky, develop hairline cracks, and lose its ability to shed water effectively.

Whether you have a lakehouse near Marble Falls or a ranch property outside Bertram, we tailor our repair approach to the specific conditions affecting your stucco. Our familiarity with Hill Country construction and materials means you get a repair that is built to perform in this environment, not just look good on day one.

What's Included With Our Stucco Repair in Burnet County

Every project includes expert craftsmanship, quality materials, and a commitment to lasting results.

On-Site Damage Assessment

We examine every exterior wall, paying close attention to areas around windows, doors, and where the stucco meets stone or wood trim common on Hill Country homes.

Moisture Evaluation

We test behind the stucco surface to determine if water has penetrated the wall and if the underlying structure needs attention.

Damaged Stucco Removal

All compromised material is removed to expose sound substrate, giving the repair a solid foundation to bond against.

Substrate Repair

Lath, wire mesh, and building paper are repaired or replaced as needed so the new stucco has a proper base.

Color & Texture Matching

We carefully match the color tone and surface texture of your existing finish so the repaired area blends naturally.

UV-Resistant Finish & Seal

The final coat is sealed with a UV-resistant treatment to withstand the intense Burnet County sun and reduce future chalking.

Common Stucco Repair Needs in Burnet County

Burnet County stucco damage tends to follow patterns tied to the local terrain and weather. Here are the issues we address most frequently.

Window & Door Frame Cracks

Even on stable limestone foundations, thermal movement and wood-frame shrinkage cause cracks to form around openings. We reinforce these joints and rebuild the stucco with flexibility built in.

Water Damage from Poor Drainage

Shallow Hill Country soils often direct rainwater toward building foundations and walls. We repair the stucco damage, and recommend drainage corrections to prevent recurrence.

Patch Failures

Quick patch jobs using mismatched material or inadequate prep fail rapidly in the intense Hill Country sun. We start fresh with proper substrate prep and compatible materials.

Delamination

Heat cycling causes the stucco to expand and contract at a different rate than the substrate, leading to separation. We remove hollow sections and rebuild them with improved bonding.

Surface Chalking & Efflorescence

UV breakdown and mineral-rich moisture migrating through limestone-adjacent walls leave chalky residue and white staining. We clean, seal, and repair the affected surfaces.

Impact & Weather Damage

Hail, windblown branches, and even deer rubbing against walls damage stucco in rural Burnet County. We restore the damaged areas to their original finish.

Why Choose Us for Stucco Repair in Burnet County?

  • We diagnose the real cause of every stucco issue — surface patches over hidden moisture problems just delay the inevitable.
  • Our materials and techniques account for the extreme UV exposure that breaks down stucco finishes in the Hill Country.
  • Every repair is color and texture matched to your existing wall so the fix is invisible once cured.
  • We fix substrate and drainage-related problems before applying new material, preventing the same issue from coming back.
  • Written warranties cover materials and workmanship on all repair projects.
  • Free inspections throughout Burnet County — call (512) 706-9699 to schedule.

Licensed & Insured

Full liability coverage and workers' compensation for every project in Burnet County.

20+ Years Experience

Decades of hands-on stucco work across Central Texas residential and commercial properties.

Certified Applicators

Certified by Sto Corp, Senergy/BASF, Master Wall, Dryvit, and Parex LaHabra for the highest quality results.

Our Stucco Repair Process

A straightforward approach from start to finish — no surprises, no shortcuts.

1

Schedule a Visit

Contact us to set up a free inspection at your Burnet County property. We serve Marble Falls, Burnet, Bertram, and the surrounding lake communities.

2

Identify the Cause

We inspect the stucco, test for moisture, and determine whether UV degradation, drainage issues, or substrate failure is behind the damage.

3

Go Over the Repair Plan

We explain what needs to be repaired, how we will do it, and what it costs. You get a written estimate with no obligation.

4

Finish the Repair

Our crew removes the bad stucco, repairs the wall behind it, and applies a sealed, matched finish. We clean up and inspect the completed work with you.
